channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: NNC Foundation Programs 2026: Scholarships, Community Grants, Literacy Support & Immigration Fee Assistance Opportunities in the United States
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The NNC Foundation (NamKil & Noul Chung Foundation) is a registered 501(c)(3) tax-exempt nonprofit organization in the United States dedicated to transforming lives through education, literacy development, and targeted financial assistance programs. Through its structured initiatives, the foundation supports students, nonprofit organizations, and individuals facing immigration-related financial barriers. In 2026, the NNC Foundation continues to […]

The post NNC Foundation Programs 2026: Scholarships, Community Grants, Literacy Support & Immigration Fee Assistance Opportunities in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The NNC Foundation (NamKil & Noul Chung Foundation) is a registered 501(c)(3) tax-exempt nonprofit organization in the United States dedicated to transforming lives through education, literacy development, and targeted financial assistance programs. Through its structured initiatives, the foundation supports students, nonprofit organizations, and individuals facing immigration-related financial barriers.

In 2026, the NNC Foundation continues to offer four active programs designed to promote educational equity, strengthen communities, and support vulnerable populations across the United States.

These programs include scholarships for students, literacy and library development initiatives, nonprofit community partnership grants, and immigration filing fee support for employment-based applicants.

Overview of NNC Foundation Mission and Impact

The mission of the NNC Foundation is centered on empowering individuals and communities through:

  • Access to education and academic opportunity
  • Promotion of literacy and lifelong learning
  • Financial support for underserved populations
  • Strengthening nonprofit-community partnerships
  • Reducing barriers in immigration processes for eligible applicants

The foundation works with students, nonprofit organizations, and individuals in need, focusing on long-term social impact and sustainable development.

Active Programs Offered by NNC Foundation (2026)

The NNC Foundation currently runs four major programs, each targeting a specific area of social and educational development.

1. Global Domestic Scholarship Program

The Global Domestic Scholarship Program is one of the foundation’s flagship initiatives designed to support academically strong students from financially challenged backgrounds.

Program Objective

This scholarship aims to reward students who demonstrate:

  • Academic excellence
  • Strong community engagement
  • Leadership potential
  • Financial need

Scholarship Value

  • High School Students: $2,000 award
  • Undergraduate & Graduate Students: $3,000 award

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet all of the following conditions:

  • Must be a U.S. citizen, permanent resident, or have valid migratory status
  • Must be currently enrolled in an accredited institution in the United States
  • Must maintain a minimum GPA of 3.0 on a 4.0 scale
  • Must demonstrate financial need and academic achievement

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

  • Essay (PDF format)
  • Most recent tax return (Form 1040)
  • Academic transcript
  • Recommendation letter

Contact

Help@nncfoundation.org

2. Literacy & Library Programs

The Literacy & Library Programs are community-focused initiatives aimed at strengthening education through reading and knowledge development.

Program Purpose

This initiative focuses on:

  • Enhancing language comprehension skills
  • Promoting reading culture among children and youth
  • Supporting local libraries and educational infrastructure
  • Encouraging lifelong learning habits

Key Impact Areas

  • Local communities across the United States
  • International educational outreach partnerships
  • School-based and public library support systems

Program Focus

  • Literacy education development
  • Library operations support
  • Reading and comprehension programs
  • Community learning enhancement

Contact

Help@nncfoundation.org

3. NNC Community Partnership Program

The Community Partnership Program provides funding and collaboration opportunities for nonprofit organizations working at the grassroots level.

Program Objective

This grant program is designed to support organizations that address:

  • Education inequality
  • Literacy improvement
  • Community health development
  • Poverty reduction
  • Social innovation initiatives

Grant Focus

  • Support for underserved and low-income populations
  • Capacity building for nonprofit organizations
  • Sustainable community development projects

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization
  • Have a mission aligned with education, health, or community development
  • Demonstrate at least 1–2 years of active community service experience

Application Status

  • Currently accepting applications

Contact

Help@nncfoundation.org

4. Immigration Filing Fee Support Grant   

The Immigration Filing Fee Support Grant provides critical financial assistance to individuals navigating employment-based immigration processes in the United States.

Program Objective

This grant is designed to reduce financial barriers by covering USCIS filing fees for eligible applicants.

Key Support Features

  • Direct payment of filing fees to USCIS on behalf of applicants
  • Financial assistance for employment-based immigration petitions
  • One-time award per beneficiary

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be a beneficiary of an employment-based immigration petition
  • Be currently in the immigration petition process
  • Demonstrate financial need
  • Provide official documentation of the petition and USCIS fee instructions

Important Notes

  • Grant payment is issued directly to USCIS
  • Only main applicants are eligible
  • Each applicant may receive only one award

Contact

Help@nncfoundation.org

About the NNC Foundation

The NamKil & Noul Chung Foundation is a U.S.-based nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing educational opportunity, community empowerment, and immigrant support services.

Organization Details

  • Status: 501(c)(3) Tax-Exempt Organization
  • Address: PO Box #3301, Duluth, GA 30096, United States
  • Phone: (470) 282-1643
  • Email: Help@nncfoundation.org

Why These Programs Matter

The NNC Foundation programs collectively aim to:

  • Reduce educational inequality
  • Support students from low-income families
  • Strengthen literacy and learning infrastructure
  • Empower nonprofit organizations
  • Provide financial relief to immigration applicants

These initiatives play a crucial role in building stronger communities and supporting long-term human development goals.

Conclusion

The NNC Foundation 2026 programs represent a strong commitment to education, community development, and immigrant support within the United States. Whether you are a student seeking financial aid, a nonprofit organization looking for funding, or an individual navigating immigration costs, these programs provide meaningful opportunities for assistance and growth.

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here  
  • More global Scholarship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post NNC Foundation Programs 2026: Scholarships, Community Grants, Literacy Support & Immigration Fee Assistance Opportunities in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: IOM Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC) Philippines Recruitment 2026: Latest Job Vacancies, Roles, Deadlines, and How to Apply
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The International Organization for Migration (IOM) continues to expand its global humanitarian and administrative operations through its Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC), also known as the Manila Administrative Centre (MAC). As part of its worldwide mission to support migration governance and humanitarian assistance, IOM is currently recruiting for multiple positions across administration, human resources, supply […]

The post IOM Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC) Philippines Recruitment 2026: Latest Job Vacancies, Roles, Deadlines, and How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Organization for Migration (IOM) continues to expand its global humanitarian and administrative operations through its Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC), also known as the Manila Administrative Centre (MAC). As part of its worldwide mission to support migration governance and humanitarian assistance, IOM is currently recruiting for multiple positions across administration, human resources, supply chain, healthcare, data analysis, payroll, and consultancy roles.

All positions advertised under this recruitment cycle are based in the Philippines, specifically within the Manila Global Administrative Centre, a strategic hub supporting IOM operations worldwide.

About the International Organization for Migration (IOM)

The International Organization for Migration (IOM) is a leading intergovernmental organization working in the field of migration management. It operates globally to support migrants, governments, and communities through humanitarian response, development programs, and policy support.

As part of the United Nations migration system, IOM emphasizes:

  • Efficiency and high professional standards
  • Integrity and accountability in humanitarian work
  • Diversity and inclusion across its global workforce
  • Equitable geographical representation in staffing
  • Multicultural collaboration in all duty stations

Working at IOM means contributing directly to life-saving operations, global migration governance, and humanitarian coordination across countries.

Overview of IOM Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC) – Manila

The Manila-based Administrative Centre serves as a global operational hub that supports:

  • Human resources management
  • Supply chain and logistics coordination
  • Medical and health data services
  • Payroll and financial administration
  • IT, data analysis, and digital transformation
  • Recruitment and talent acquisition

This center plays a key role in ensuring that IOM field missions operate efficiently across continents.

Latest Job Vacancies at IOM GSSC (2026)

Below are the currently open positions, including deadlines and grades:

1. Asset and Inventory Assistant (G-4)

  • Code: VN 047/2026
  • Deadline: 22 May 2026
  • Role: Manage inventory systems, asset tracking, and logistics documentation.

2. HR Associate (Ungraded)

  • Code: CFA 005/2026
  • Deadline: 22 May 2026
  • Role: Support HR processes, recruitment administration, and employee records.

3. National Medical Officer (Ungraded)

  • Code: CFA 006/2026
  • Deadline: 24 May 2026
  • Role: Provide medical expertise for staff health and humanitarian operations.

4. Human Resources Intern (Open to Fresh Graduates)

  • Code: INT 007/2026
  • Deadline: 29 May 2026
  • Role: Entry-level HR support and administrative learning opportunity.

5. Supply Chain Associate (Master Data Management) (G-5)

  • Code: VN 048/2026
  • Deadline: 29 May 2026
  • Role: Manage supply chain data systems and procurement support.

6. National Teleradiology Data Analysis Officer (NO-A)

  • Code: VN 049/2026
  • Deadline: 29 May 2026
  • Role: Analyze medical imaging data and support remote diagnostics systems.

7. Senior Payroll Administrator (Ungraded)

  • Code: CFA 007-008/2026
  • Deadline: 04 June 2026
  • Role: Oversee payroll systems, salary processing, and financial compliance.

8. National HR Officer (Talent Acquisition Regional Focal) (NO-B)

  • Code: VN 050/2026
  • Deadline: 04 June 2026
  • Role: Lead recruitment strategies and regional talent acquisition processes.

9. SEO Specialist (G-6)

  • Code: VN 031/2026
  • Deadline: 04 June 2026
  • Role: Optimize digital visibility, web content strategy, and search engine performance.

10. Radiologist (Teleradiology) Consultant

  • Code: CON 011-018/2026
  • Deadline: 05 June 2026
  • Role: Provide expert radiology consultation through remote diagnostic systems.

Key Features of Working with IOM GSSC

Working within the Manila Administrative Centre offers:

  • International exposure in a UN-related environment
  • Multidisciplinary career development opportunities
  • Competitive and structured global salary scales
  • Experience in humanitarian and migration-focused operations
  • Multicultural and inclusive workplace culture
  • Opportunities for career progression within the UN system

How to Apply

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply directly through the official IOM careers platform by submitting:

  • Updated CV/resume
  • Cover letter tailored to the position
  • Academic certificates and relevant professional documents
  • Proof of experience (where required)

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Why This Opportunity Matters

These vacancies represent a significant opportunity to:

  • Join a UN-affiliated organization
  • Contribute to global humanitarian and migration governance
  • Work in a structured international administrative environment
  • Build a career in HR, logistics, healthcare, data analysis, or digital systems

Conclusion

The IOM GSSC Manila recruitment drive for 2026 offers diverse career pathways for professionals and fresh graduates alike. With roles ranging from internships to senior consultancy positions, this is a key opportunity for individuals seeking international careers within the United Nations system.

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post IOM Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC) Philippines Recruitment 2026: Latest Job Vacancies, Roles, Deadlines, and How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Vision Brussels Careers 2026: Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist and Junior Communication & Policy Officer Opportunities in Belgium
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
World Vision International has announced exciting career opportunities within its European Union Representation Office (EUREP) in Brussels, Belgium. These openings provide professionals and young graduates with the opportunity to contribute to global humanitarian advocacy, European Union policy engagement, child-focused development work, and international communications. The organization is currently recruiting for two important positions: Humanitarian Advocacy […]

The post World Vision Brussels Careers 2026: Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist and Junior Communication & Policy Officer Opportunities in Belgium appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
World Vision International has announced exciting career opportunities within its European Union Representation Office (EUREP) in Brussels, Belgium. These openings provide professionals and young graduates with the opportunity to contribute to global humanitarian advocacy, European Union policy engagement, child-focused development work, and international communications.

The organization is currently recruiting for two important positions:

  1. Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist
  2. Junior Communication & Policy Officer (Trainee)

Both opportunities are based in Brussels and offer candidates the chance to work with one of the world’s largest humanitarian and development organizations while engaging directly with European Union institutions, advocacy networks, and global policy initiatives.

These positions are ideal for individuals passionate about humanitarian affairs, development cooperation, communications, public policy, advocacy, children’s rights, and international relations.

About World Vision

World Vision International is a global Christian humanitarian, development, and advocacy organization dedicated to improving the lives of vulnerable children, families, and communities worldwide.

Operating in nearly 100 countries with more than 31,000 staff members, World Vision works across areas such as:

  • Child protection
  • Emergency response
  • Humanitarian assistance
  • Poverty reduction
  • Education
  • Health and nutrition
  • Advocacy and policy engagement
  • Community resilience
  • Development programming

The organization collaborates with governments, institutions, NGOs, donors, and local communities to create sustainable and impactful solutions to global challenges affecting children and vulnerable populations.

About the World Vision EU Representation Office (EUREP)

The Brussels-based EU Representation Office supports World Vision’s engagement with European Union institutions and stakeholders.

The office focuses on two major strategic priorities:

1. Influencing EU Policies and Decision-Making

The office works to ensure that European Union policies, funding programs, and political decisions positively impact vulnerable children and communities around the world.

This involves:

  • Policy advocacy
  • Humanitarian diplomacy
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Campaign coordination
  • Strategic communication
  • Institutional partnerships

2. Facilitating Access to EU Funding Opportunities

The office also supports World Vision teams globally in accessing EU development and humanitarian funding opportunities.

As one of the largest international donors, the European Union plays a critical role in financing global humanitarian and development initiatives. World Vision’s Brussels office helps connect field offices and programs with these funding mechanisms.

Position 1: Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist

Job Location

Brussels, Belgium

Employment Type

Permanent full-time contract

Department

Humanitarian Team

Start Date

As soon as possible (ASAP)

Overview of the Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist Role

The Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist will play a central role in supporting World Vision’s humanitarian engagement with European Union institutions.

The selected candidate will contribute to:

  • EU humanitarian advocacy strategies
  • Policy analysis
  • Institutional engagement
  • Humanitarian diplomacy
  • Funding environment monitoring
  • Child-focused humanitarian action

The role focuses heavily on humanitarian crises, resilience, fragility, and Nexus-related issues affecting vulnerable populations globally.

The successful candidate will help strengthen World Vision’s visibility and credibility within European humanitarian policy spaces while supporting advocacy initiatives linked to children in crisis and fragile contexts.

Key Responsibilities

The Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist will undertake several strategic and operational responsibilities.

Policy and Advocacy Strategy

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting the development and implementation of EU humanitarian advocacy strategies
  • Aligning advocacy efforts with World Vision priorities and ECHO priorities
  • Coordinating humanitarian policy initiatives

Humanitarian Advocacy Leadership

The role involves leading advocacy actions concerning:

  • Humanitarian crises
  • Fragility
  • Resilience
  • Nexus-related issues

Research and Policy Documentation

The selected candidate will:

  • Draft policy papers
  • Prepare advocacy briefs
  • Develop analyses and position papers
  • Produce documents related to EU humanitarian affairs

EU Policy Monitoring

Responsibilities include monitoring:

  • EU humanitarian policies
  • Political trends
  • Stakeholder developments
  • Funding environments

Stakeholder Engagement

The specialist will strengthen relationships with:

  • ECHO
  • EU institutions
  • NGOs
  • Coalitions
  • Humanitarian partners in Brussels

Representation Duties

The role also includes representing World Vision at:

  • EU humanitarian events
  • Advocacy forums
  • Policy discussions
  • Networks such as VOICE

Coordination and Internal Advisory Support

The candidate will:

  • Coordinate advocacy visits and campaigns
  • Support high-level engagements
  • Advise internal teams on EU humanitarian advocacy priorities
  • Facilitate alignment across global, regional, and field offices

Qualifications and Skills Required

Candidates applying for this role should possess strong academic, professional, and interpersonal skills.

Educational Requirements

Applicants should hold a Master’s degree in areas such as:

  • International Relations
  • Humanitarian Affairs
  • Public Policy
  • Development Studies
  • Related disciplines

Professional Experience

Candidates should have:

  • 2 to 4 years of experience in EU humanitarian policy and advocacy

Technical Knowledge

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong understanding of EU institutions
  • Strategic thinking abilities
  • Policy influence capabilities
  • Advocacy coordination experience

Additional Skills

Required competencies include:

  • Networking and coalition-building
  • Project coordination
  • Excellent organizational skills
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities
  • Strong communication abilities

Language Requirements

  • Excellent written and spoken English is mandatory
  • French is considered an advantage

Other Requirements

Candidates should:

  • Be willing to travel occasionally
  • Support diversity, equity, and inclusion
  • Commit to World Vision’s mission and values
  • Already possess the legal right to work in Belgium

Salary and Benefits

World Vision offers an attractive compensation package for the Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist role.

Salary Range

  • €3,039 to €3,490 gross per month
  • Paid over 13.92 months

Additional Benefits

Employees will benefit from:

  • Permanent full-time contract
  • Supportive work environment
  • Inclusive and dynamic team culture
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Respectful workplace environment
  • Competitive market-aligned compensation

Application Deadline for Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist

Interested candidates must submit their:

  • CV
  • Cover letter

Applications should be sent to:

  • eulo-info@wvi.org

Subject line:

  • “Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist”

Deadline

31 May 2026

Position 2: Junior Communication & Policy Officer (Trainee)

Job Location

Brussels, Belgium

Employment Type

Paid internship under Contrat d’Immersion Professionnelle (CIP)

Department

Advocacy Team

Start Date

As soon as possible (ASAP)

Overview of the Junior Communication & Policy Officer Role

The Junior Communication & Policy Officer position is designed for young professionals seeking experience in:

  • Communication
  • Advocacy
  • Policy engagement
  • Event coordination
  • Digital communications
  • EU institutional relations

The trainee will contribute to communication activities and support advocacy and policy initiatives related to children’s rights and development priorities.

This role offers increasing levels of responsibility and autonomy throughout the traineeship period.

Key Responsibilities

Communication Support

The trainee will:

  • Ensure communication outputs comply with branding and visibility requirements
  • Support strategic communication initiatives
  • Assist in campaign development
  • Create communication materials

Digital Communications

Responsibilities include:

  • Managing digital communication channels
  • Updating websites
  • Supporting social media activities
  • Creating online content

Monitoring and Analysis

The candidate will:

  • Monitor communication activities
  • Analyze communication impact
  • Support reporting and evaluation efforts

Event Coordination

The role also includes:

  • Assisting with event planning
  • Coordinating meetings
  • Supporting advocacy activities

Qualifications and Skills Required

Educational Background

Candidates should possess:

  • A Master’s degree in Communication or related fields

OR

  • Relevant experience linked to World Vision’s mission

Knowledge Requirements

Applicants should understand:

  • European Union institutions
  • Development policies
  • Child-focused advocacy

Technical Skills

Required competencies include:

  • Digital communication skills
  • Social media management
  • Research and analytical skills
  • Communication performance monitoring
  • Policy analysis

Language Requirements

  • Excellent written and spoken English

Personal Skills

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong organizational skills
  • Ability to meet deadlines
  • Team spirit
  • Capacity to work in multicultural environments
  • Support for World Vision’s Christian identity and values

Internship Benefits

The Junior Communication & Policy Officer internship includes several attractive benefits.

Internship Duration

  • 6 months
  • Possibility of extension for another 6 months

Additional Benefits

Interns will receive:

  • Competitive internship compensation
  • Lunch vouchers
  • 5 weeks of paid holiday
  • Belgian public holidays
  • Up to 20 days of remote work from abroad

The organization also promotes:

  • Personal development
  • Team spirit
  • Autonomy
  • Inclusive workplace culture

Application Deadline for Junior Communication & Policy Officer

Applicants must submit:

  • CV
  • Cover letter

Email:

  • eulo-info@wvi.org

Subject line:

  • “Junior Communication & Policy Officer (Trainee)”

Deadline

22 May 2026

Why These Opportunities Matter

These positions provide exceptional opportunities for professionals and graduates interested in:

  • European Union policy
  • Humanitarian affairs
  • International advocacy
  • Child rights
  • Development cooperation
  • Global communications

Working within World Vision’s EU Representation Office offers direct exposure to high-level policy discussions, international partnerships, and humanitarian advocacy processes that shape global development agendas.

Candidates will gain valuable experience engaging with:

  • EU institutions
  • NGOs
  • Donors
  • Advocacy coalitions
  • Humanitarian networks

Final Thoughts

The 2026 recruitment opportunities at World Vision International offer talented professionals and aspiring advocates the chance to build meaningful careers while contributing to global humanitarian and child-focused development efforts.

Whether applying for the permanent Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist role or the Junior Communication & Policy Officer traineeship, candidates will join a globally respected organization dedicated to creating positive change for vulnerable communities worldwide.

Professionals with strong backgrounds in advocacy, communication, humanitarian affairs, EU policy, and international development should strongly consider applying before the respective deadlines.

 

  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

 

The post World Vision Brussels Careers 2026: Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist and Junior Communication & Policy Officer Opportunities in Belgium appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature 2026–2027: A Global Opportunity Transforming Mental Health Innovation
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The global mental health crisis continues to affect millions of people across every continent, creating an urgent need for innovative, evidence-based solutions that can improve lives and strengthen healthcare systems. In response to this growing challenge, Wellcome, in partnership with Nature, has launched the prestigious Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature. Recognized as […]

The post Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature 2026–2027: A Global Opportunity Transforming Mental Health Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The global mental health crisis continues to affect millions of people across every continent, creating an urgent need for innovative, evidence-based solutions that can improve lives and strengthen healthcare systems. In response to this growing challenge, Wellcome, in partnership with Nature, has launched the prestigious Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature.

Recognized as one of the world’s most significant mental health research prizes, this initiative aims to accelerate transformative interventions targeting anxiety, depression, and psychosis. The competition not only provides substantial financial support but also offers global recognition, policy visibility, networking opportunities, and practical assistance to help successful interventions move toward large-scale adoption.

Applications are now open for the 2026–2027 edition, with one winning team set to receive an extraordinary US$1 million prize, while three finalists will each receive US$250,000.

About the Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature

The Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature was created to recognize and support groundbreaking mental health interventions capable of producing measurable and lasting impacts on people living with mental health conditions.

The prize specifically focuses on interventions addressing:

  • Anxiety
  • Depression
  • Psychosis

Mental health disorders remain among the leading causes of disability worldwide. Despite increasing awareness, access to effective treatments and scalable interventions remains limited in many regions. Through this initiative, Wellcome and Nature aim to bridge the gap between scientific discovery and real-world implementation.

The prize celebrates innovation while also helping researchers and institutions overcome barriers related to visibility, funding, adoption, and policy integration.

Main Goals of the Prize

The Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature was established with several ambitious objectives designed to reshape the future of global mental health research and implementation.

1. Accelerate Effective Mental Health Interventions

The prize seeks to support interventions that demonstrate effectiveness in improving outcomes for individuals affected by anxiety, depression, or psychosis.

This includes innovations related to:

  • Clinical treatments
  • Therapeutic models
  • Community-based interventions
  • Digital mental health tools
  • Behavioral approaches
  • Preventive strategies
  • Public health solutions
  • Technology-driven mental health systems

The ultimate aim is to help these interventions move more rapidly toward widespread adoption.

2. Increase Policy Prioritization of Mental Health

Another major objective is to encourage governments, institutions, and health systems to prioritize mental health within public policy and healthcare planning.

Winning projects may influence:

  • National mental health strategies
  • Global health frameworks
  • Healthcare investment decisions
  • Research funding priorities
  • Community mental health programs

3. Inspire Global Interest in Mental Health Science

The prize also aims to increase public and institutional enthusiasm for mental health research by highlighting innovative scientific achievements capable of transforming lives.

By elevating successful interventions, the initiative hopes to encourage:

  • Greater public awareness
  • Increased philanthropic investment
  • Expanded scientific collaboration
  • More funding for mental health research worldwide

Prize Benefits and Financial Awards

One of the most attractive aspects of this opportunity is its substantial financial support.

Overall Winner

The winning team will receive:

  • US$1 million

This funding is intended to help accelerate the implementation and expansion of the winning intervention.

Finalists

Three runner-up teams will each receive:

  • US$250,000

These funds are designed to support further development, adoption, and policy integration of finalist interventions.

Additional Benefits Beyond Funding

The Wellcome Prize offers far more than financial rewards. Selected teams will also gain access to several strategic advantages that can significantly increase the impact of their work.

Global Visibility

Winning and finalist teams will receive international recognition through the extensive networks of Wellcome and Nature.

Strategic Connections

Participants may benefit from networking opportunities involving:

  • Policymakers
  • Researchers
  • Healthcare leaders
  • Investors
  • Philanthropic organizations
  • Global health institutions

Practical Support

The initiative also provides practical support aimed at helping interventions progress toward real-world adoption and integration into healthcare systems.

Enhanced Credibility

Being associated with internationally respected organizations like Wellcome and Nature can significantly strengthen the credibility and influence of participating teams and institutions.

Who Can Apply?

The Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature is open to research teams developing innovative interventions related to anxiety, depression, or psychosis.

Eligible teams may come from various sectors, including:

  • Universities
  • Research institutes
  • Hospitals
  • Government organizations
  • Not-for-profit organizations
  • Small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs)

Importantly, applications are welcomed from teams based anywhere in the world, making this a truly global opportunity.

Eligible Research Areas

Applicants may submit interventions related to a wide range of mental health innovation areas, provided they focus on anxiety, depression, or psychosis.

Examples may include:

  • Novel treatment methods
  • Community mental health programs
  • AI-driven mental health technologies
  • Telehealth solutions
  • Preventive mental health interventions
  • Behavioral therapies
  • Neurobiological research applications
  • Digital mental health platforms
  • Public health mental health strategies
  • Early diagnosis systems
  • Scalable healthcare delivery models

Projects demonstrating strong scientific evidence and real-world impact potential are likely to stand out during the evaluation process.

Important Dates

Applicants should carefully note the following timeline for the 2026–2027 competition cycle.

Event Date
Applications Open 14 May 2026
Application Deadline 18 September 2026
Shortlist Announcement April 2027
Finalists Announcement May 2027
Winner Announcement June 2027

About the Organizers

Wellcome

Wellcome is a globally recognized charitable foundation supporting scientific research aimed at solving urgent health challenges worldwide.

The organization focuses on:

  • Mental health
  • Infectious diseases
  • Climate and health
  • Life sciences
  • Human wellbeing

Wellcome invests heavily in discovery research and global health innovation initiatives.

Nature

Nature is one of the world’s leading scientific publishing organizations, known for advancing scientific discovery through high-quality research publications and authoritative journalism.

Through Nature Awards, the organization supports outstanding researchers and promotes scientific innovation capable of generating real-world impact.

Why This Prize Matters Globally

Mental health conditions continue to rise worldwide, affecting individuals, families, healthcare systems, and economies. Despite increasing awareness, many innovative interventions struggle to obtain the funding, policy attention, and implementation support necessary to scale their impact.

The Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature addresses this gap directly by:

  • Rewarding evidence-based innovation
  • Encouraging policy prioritization
  • Increasing global awareness
  • Supporting scalable mental health solutions
  • Accelerating real-world implementation

For research teams working on transformative mental health interventions, this prize represents a rare opportunity to gain both substantial funding and international recognition.

How to Apply

Interested teams should prepare a strong application showcasing:

  • The innovation behind their intervention
  • Scientific evidence of effectiveness
  • Potential for real-world impact
  • Scalability and adoption potential
  • Relevance to anxiety, depression, or psychosis

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before the official deadline of 18 September 2026.

Official Website: Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature

Final Thoughts

The Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature stands out as one of the most ambitious and impactful mental health science competitions in the world. By combining major financial support with global visibility and policy engagement opportunities, the prize aims to accelerate innovations capable of transforming mental healthcare worldwide.

For researchers, institutions, hospitals, nonprofits, and SMEs developing effective interventions for anxiety, depression, or psychosis, this opportunity could become a powerful catalyst for scaling impact and influencing the future of global mental health.

 

Apply here  

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Award  opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature 2026–2027: A Global Opportunity Transforming Mental Health Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: TOKAS Research Residency Program 2027 in Tokyo, Japan | Fully Supported Arts & Culture Research Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The TOKAS Residency has officially announced the Research Residency Program 2027, an international opportunity designed for artists, researchers, and cultural practitioners to conduct in-depth research on arts and culture in Tokyo, Japan. This prestigious residency program invites both international and local creators to engage in independent research while being immersed in one of the world’s […]

The post TOKAS Research Residency Program 2027 in Tokyo, Japan | Fully Supported Arts & Culture Research Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The TOKAS Residency has officially announced the Research Residency Program 2027, an international opportunity designed for artists, researchers, and cultural practitioners to conduct in-depth research on arts and culture in Tokyo, Japan.

This prestigious residency program invites both international and local creators to engage in independent research while being immersed in one of the world’s most dynamic cultural cities.

Selected participants will receive accommodation support and the opportunity to develop research-based artistic and cultural projects over a residency period of 6 to 12 weeks.

About the TOKAS Research Residency Program

The Research Residency Program is part of TOKAS’ mission to support global artistic exchange and cultural research.

It provides a structured environment for creators to:

  • Explore arts and cultural practices in Tokyo
  • Conduct field-based research
  • Develop new artistic or academic projects
  • Engage with Japan’s creative ecosystem
  • Build international cultural connections

The program is designed to support diverse creative disciplines and encourage cross-cultural dialogue between Japan and the global arts community.

Residency Duration and Schedule

Selected creators will participate in a 6 to 12-week residency during one of the following periods:

Residency Period Options

  • May – July 2027
  • September – November 2027
  • January – March 2028

Participants will be assigned a period by the programme (it cannot be selected by the applicant).

Number of Selected Creators

  • Only 5 creators will be selected globally

This makes the program highly competitive and prestigious.

Fields of Study and Creative Disciplines

The residency is open to a wide range of artistic and cultural disciplines, including:

  • Visual Arts
  • Design
  • Architecture
  • Music
  • Performing Arts
  • Curation
  • Art Criticism
  • Cultural Research
  • Cultural Writing

The program strongly encourages interdisciplinary approaches that combine creative practice with research.

What the Residency Provides

Selected participants will receive essential support to carry out their research in Tokyo.

Provided Support

  • Living space (single-room accommodation)
  • Access to Tokyo’s cultural environment
  • Time and space for independent research
  • Institutional support from TOKAS Residency

Not Included

  • Studio or dedicated workspace is NOT provided
  • Additional travel within Japan is NOT funded
  • Family accommodation is NOT included
  • External living or travel costs are not supported

Application Requirements

Applicants must prepare and submit the following materials:

Required Documents

  • Completed application form (PDF format)
  • Portfolio (maximum 6 pages for duo applicants)
  • Two letters of recommendation
  • Online submission form

Application Process

  1. Download the official application package
  2. Complete and fill in the application form using Adobe Reader
  3. Prepare portfolio and supporting documents
  4. Submit online via the official application portal

Eligibility Criteria

General Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be professional creators in arts and culture fields
  • Demonstrate significant experience in their discipline
  • Have a strong research or artistic proposal
  • Be able to conduct residency-based research in Tokyo

Student Eligibility

  • Must NOT be a student during residency participation
  • Must have substantial professional experience in their field

Duo Applications

  • Only eligible if applicants have previously worked together
  • Must submit a shared portfolio and documentation

Application Timeline (2027 Cycle)

Key Dates

  • Application Period Opens: May 19, 2026
  • Application Deadline: June 16, 2026 (18:00 JST)
  • Residency Periods: 2027–early 2028 (multiple cycles)
  • Results and selections follow internal review process

Deadline

The application deadline for the TOKAS Research Residency Program 2027 is:

June 16, 2026 (18:00 JST)

How to Apply

Click here for more and to apply

Conclusion

The TOKAS Research Residency Program 2027 is a rare and highly competitive opportunity for artists and cultural researchers to immerse themselves in Tokyo’s vibrant creative environment.

With only five selected participants worldwide, the program offers an exclusive platform for deep artistic research, cultural exchange, and professional development in one of the world’s leading cultural capitals.

For creators seeking to expand their artistic practice through research and international experience, this residency offers a transformative opportunity.

Click here for more global fellowship opportunities

The post TOKAS Research Residency Program 2027 in Tokyo, Japan | Fully Supported Arts & Culture Research Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Career Development Fellowship in International Relations and Security at Durham University 2026 | Fully Structured Academic Opportunity in the UK
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Career Development Fellowship in International Relations and Security (Job Number: 26000640) offered by Durham University represents a prestigious early-career academic opportunity designed to support emerging scholars in building a strong foundation in teaching, research, and academic leadership within one of the United Kingdom’s most respected institutions. Hosted within the School of Government and International […]

The post Career Development Fellowship in International Relations and Security at Durham University 2026 | Fully Structured Academic Opportunity in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Career Development Fellowship in International Relations and Security (Job Number: 26000640) offered by Durham University represents a prestigious early-career academic opportunity designed to support emerging scholars in building a strong foundation in teaching, research, and academic leadership within one of the United Kingdom’s most respected institutions.

Hosted within the School of Government and International Affairs, this fellowship focuses on advancing research and teaching in International Relations (IR), Security Studies, post-colonial IR perspectives, conflict and violence studies, and transitional justice. It is structured as a 36-month fixed-term, full-time academic position, providing structured mentorship, professional development, and teaching experience.

This opportunity is particularly significant for early-career researchers seeking to transition into permanent academic roles in leading global universities.

About Durham University and Its Academic Excellence

Durham University is globally recognized for its excellence in research, education, and academic impact. Located in a historic UNESCO World Heritage city in the United Kingdom, the university is known for:

  • High-impact global research contributions
  • Strong interdisciplinary academic communities
  • World-class teaching standards
  • A supportive environment for early-career researchers

Within the university, the School of Government and International Affairs (SGIA) is a leading academic hub specializing in politics, international relations, global security, and public policy research. The fellowship is embedded within this intellectual ecosystem, offering access to internationally renowned scholars and research groups such as the Durham Global Security Institute.

Overview of the Career Development Fellowship

The Career Development Fellowship is designed as a structured academic training and development programme for early-career scholars in International Relations and Security Studies.

Key Features of the Fellowship

  • Duration: 36 months (fixed-term)
  • Employment Type: Full-time (35 hours per week)
  • Academic Level: Grade 7 position
  • Salary Range: £38,784 – £46,079 per annum
  • Start Date: Expected 1 September 2026

The fellowship combines research, teaching, and academic service responsibilities, ensuring that fellows develop a well-rounded academic portfolio suitable for future permanent academic positions.

Purpose of the Fellowship

The primary aim of this fellowship is to support early-career academics in:

  • Developing internationally excellent research outputs
  • Building teaching experience at undergraduate and postgraduate levels
  • Gaining academic leadership and administrative skills
  • Preparing for future tenure-track or permanent academic roles

The role is developmental, meaning it emphasizes mentorship, training, and structured academic growth rather than permanent employment guarantees.

Academic Focus Areas

Applicants are expected to demonstrate expertise or strong interest in one or more of the following areas:

  • International Relations theory and practice
  • Security Studies
  • Post-colonial approaches to International Relations
  • Conflict and violence studies
  • Transitional justice
  • Global politics and international governance

The university places emphasis on academic excellence, methodological diversity, and innovative research approaches rather than restrictive specialization.

Key Responsibilities of the Fellowship

Successful candidates will engage in a combination of academic duties across three main pillars:

1. Teaching and Education

Fellows will:

  • Teach undergraduate and postgraduate modules
  • Develop innovative teaching materials and curriculum content
  • Support inclusive learning environments
  • Supervise or co-supervise student research projects
  • Provide academic feedback, assessment, and student support

2. Research and Scholarship

Fellows will:

  • Develop independent research agendas aligned with departmental priorities
  • Produce high-quality, internationally recognized academic outputs
  • Contribute to research grant applications and funding proposals
  • Engage with national and international research networks
  • Strengthen the department’s research profile

3. Academic Citizenship and Development

Fellows will:

  • Participate in departmental committees
  • Contribute to institutional academic life
  • Engage in professional development programmes
  • Work towards Higher Education Academy (HEA) membership
  • Collaborate with colleagues across disciplines

Structured Academic Development Support

One of the most important aspects of this fellowship is the tailored career development support provided to all fellows.

This includes:

  • A personalized academic development plan
  • Dedicated mentorship from senior faculty members
  • Access to professional training courses
  • Leadership development opportunities
  • Protected time for research activities

This structured approach ensures that fellows are well-prepared for competitive academic job markets globally.

Eligibility Criteria

Academic Qualifications

Applicants must:

  • Hold a good first degree in a relevant discipline
  • Be within approximately 8 years of completing a PhD (or nearing completion)
  • Have a PhD in International Relations, Politics, or a related field

Career breaks due to parental leave, health conditions, or other valid reasons are considered.

Skills and Competencies Required

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong potential for producing high-quality research outputs
  • Ability to deliver engaging and effective teaching
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Commitment to equality, diversity, and inclusion
  • Capacity to contribute to academic administration and departmental activities
  • Evidence of academic promise in research or teaching excellence

Inclusion, Diversity, and Accessibility Commitment

Durham University strongly emphasizes equality, diversity, and inclusion. The institution actively supports:

  • Candidates from underrepresented backgrounds
  • Applicants with disabilities or neurodiversity
  • Flexible and accessible recruitment processes
  • Reasonable workplace adjustments

The university promotes a respectful academic environment where all individuals can thrive regardless of identity or background.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following documents:

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Covering Letter detailing experience and suitability
  • Personal Research Plan (maximum 2 pages)

All materials must clearly demonstrate alignment with the fellowship’s academic expectations.

Selection and Interview Process

  • Shortlisting based on essential criteria
  • Academic presentation to staff and students
  • Formal interview process (typically over two days)
  • Interviews expected: 6–7 July 2026

Successful candidates will be expected to begin the role by 1 September 2026.

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: 09 June 2026 (1:59 AM)
  • Interview Dates: 6–7 July 2026
  • Start Date: 1 September 2026
  • Contract Duration: 36 months

Benefits of the Fellowship

This fellowship offers a wide range of academic and professional benefits, including:

  • Competitive academic salary
  • 30 days annual leave plus public holidays
  • Pension scheme enrollment
  • Comprehensive research support
  • Teaching experience at a top UK university
  • Access to global academic networks
  • Professional development training
  • Strong career progression pathway

Apply Now

Conclusion

The Career Development Fellowship in International Relations and Security at Durham University is an outstanding opportunity for early-career academics aiming to advance their careers in global politics and security studies. With structured mentorship, strong research support, and teaching opportunities, this fellowship provides a powerful platform for academic growth and international recognition.

Applicants with strong research potential and a commitment to teaching excellence are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Career Development Fellowship in International Relations and Security at Durham University 2026 | Fully Structured Academic Opportunity in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Work4Progress Ghana 2026: Call for Collaborative Networks to Drive Job Creation and Inclusive Innovation in Afram Plains
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Work4Progress Ghana 2026 Call for Collaborative Networks is a major international development initiative designed to stimulate job creation, entrepreneurship, and inclusive economic transformation in Ghana’s Afram Plains region. Implemented by the la Caixa Foundation in partnership with GBHub Africa, this programme seeks to bring together multi-organization networks to co-create sustainable employment solutions for vulnerable […]

The post Work4Progress Ghana 2026: Call for Collaborative Networks to Drive Job Creation and Inclusive Innovation in Afram Plains appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Work4Progress Ghana 2026 Call for Collaborative Networks is a major international development initiative designed to stimulate job creation, entrepreneurship, and inclusive economic transformation in Ghana’s Afram Plains region. Implemented by the la Caixa Foundation in partnership with GBHub Africa, this programme seeks to bring together multi-organization networks to co-create sustainable employment solutions for vulnerable women and young people.

This initiative is part of the broader Work4Progress programme, which has already been successfully implemented in countries such as India, Mozambique, Colombia, and Peru, supporting over 30,000 enterprises and creating more than 70,000 jobs globally.

In Ghana, the focus is on the Afram Plains, a high-potential agricultural zone with significant opportunities for rural transformation, agribusiness expansion, and digital inclusion.

Overview of the Work4Progress Ghana Initiative

Work4Progress is an innovation-driven employment programme that strengthens local ecosystems of change through collaboration, experimentation, and scaling of solutions. The Ghana edition is structured around building collaborative networks of 3 to 5 organizations that will jointly design and implement employment-generating interventions.

The programme emphasizes:

  • Community-centered innovation
  • Inclusive economic growth
  • Sustainable job creation models
  • Strong ecosystem collaboration

Selected networks will work closely with local communities to identify needs and co-develop solutions that can be tested, refined, and scaled.

Objective of the Call for Collaborative Networks

The primary goal of this call is to select multi-stakeholder consortia (3–5 organizations each) that will:

  1. Conduct community listening exercises in Afram Plains
  2. Map economic opportunities and local value chains
  3. Co-create job creation solutions with communities
  4. Prototype and test innovative employment models
  5. Scale successful interventions for broader impact

This approach ensures that solutions are locally grounded, sustainable, and scalable.

Priority Areas of Focus

Applicants are expected to design interventions aligned with at least one or more of the following strategic areas:

1. Circular Economy and Green Jobs

  • Waste reduction initiatives
  • Recycling-based enterprises
  • Climate-smart agricultural innovations

2. E-Commerce and Digital Transformation

  • Digital marketplaces for rural producers
  • Online trade platforms for SMEs
  • Digital skills development

3. Small Enterprise Integration into Supply Chains

  • Strengthening market access for local businesses
  • Improving logistics and distribution systems
  • Building partnerships with larger buyers

4. Inclusive Business Competitiveness Technologies

  • Productivity-enhancing technologies
  • Mobile-based agricultural tools
  • Smart farming systems

5. Agricultural Value Chains Development

  • Focus on pepper, onion, and spice production
  • Post-harvest processing and packaging
  • Agribusiness scaling initiatives

Eligibility Criteria

Network Requirements

  • Applications must be submitted by a network of 3 to 5 organizations
  • Networks must jointly design and implement interventions

Lead Organization Requirements

The lead organization must:

  • Be a nonprofit organization
  • Provide recent audited financial statements
  • Have at least 5 years of experience in one or more of the following:
    • Job creation
    • Innovation
    • Agricultural development
    • International cooperation

If the lead organization is international:

  • It must include Ghanaian partner organizations
  • Must demonstrate at least 3 years of operational experience in Ghana

Eligible Partner Organizations

The following types of organizations can participate:

  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • Community-based organizations (CBOs)
  • Incubators and accelerators
  • Social enterprises
  • Academic and research institutions
  • Agricultural production and processing companies
  • Microfinance institutions
  • Impact investment organizations
  • Private companies with verified social impact mandates

Note: Public authorities are generally not eligible unless they have mixed ownership and a defined social impact mandate.

Key Documents Required

Applicants must prepare and submit the following:

  • Application Form Questions
  • Terms of Reference (ToR)
  • Budget Template
  • Responsible Statement (Annex III)
  • Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Timeline and Important Dates

  • Call Opens: 6 May 2026
  • Info Session: 12 May 2026 (MS Teams)
  • Application Deadline: 3 June 2026
  • Evaluation Period: To be confirmed
  • Notification of Results: To be confirmed

Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare early to ensure compliance with submission requirements.

Application Process

Interested networks must submit their application through the official application portal provided by the programme.

Apply Now, click HERE

Why This Opportunity Matters

This initiative is not just a funding call—it is a system-building innovation programme designed to:

  • Strengthen rural economies in Ghana
  • Create sustainable livelihoods for women and youth
  • Enhance agricultural productivity and value chain integration
  • Promote digital and green transformation in rural communities
  • Build long-term innovation ecosystems

By focusing on collaboration between multiple organizations, the programme ensures that diverse expertise is combined to solve complex development challenges.

Conclusion

The Work4Progress Ghana 2026 call represents a significant opportunity for organizations working in development, agriculture, innovation, and social impact. With its strong emphasis on collaboration, experimentation, and scaling, the programme is designed to unlock meaningful and sustainable job creation in one of Ghana’s most promising agricultural regions.

Organizations with the right expertise, networks, and commitment to social impact are encouraged to apply before the deadline.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Work4Progress Ghana 2026: Call for Collaborative Networks to Drive Job Creation and Inclusive Innovation in Afram Plains appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: SNF Global Center Research Fellowship 2026: Up to $550,000 for Child and Adolescent Mental Health Research in LMICs
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Child Mind Institute has officially opened the 2026 cycle of the prestigious SNF Global Center Research Fellowship Program, a major international fellowship opportunity supporting early-career researchers in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs). The fellowship is designed to strengthen child and adolescent mental health research globally by empowering talented researchers to lead impactful projects that […]

The post SNF Global Center Research Fellowship 2026: Up to $550,000 for Child and Adolescent Mental Health Research in LMICs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Child Mind Institute has officially opened the 2026 cycle of the prestigious SNF Global Center Research Fellowship Program, a major international fellowship opportunity supporting early-career researchers in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs).

The fellowship is designed to strengthen child and adolescent mental health research globally by empowering talented researchers to lead impactful projects that address urgent mental health challenges affecting young people in their countries and regions.

Selected fellows may receive funding of up to US$550,000 over four to five years to support research activities, professional development, international collaboration, and academic advancement.

The programme is part of the SNF Global Center for Child and Adolescent Mental Health and aims to build sustainable mental health research leadership in underserved regions around the world.

About the SNF Global Center Research Fellowship

The SNF Global Center Research Fellowship provides a long-term platform for early-career scholars working in child and adolescent mental health research to grow as independent research leaders.

The fellowship focuses on supporting researchers who are:

  • Addressing locally relevant mental health challenges
  • Conducting innovative and impactful research
  • Building evidence-based mental health solutions
  • Strengthening academic research systems in LMICs

Fellows remain based at their home institutions while receiving international exposure, mentorship, and collaboration opportunities with global experts in mental health research.

The programme creates a bridge between local impact and international scientific collaboration.

Fellowship Funding and Benefits

Selected fellows receive substantial financial and professional support designed to help them conduct high-impact research while advancing their careers.

Funding Amount

Successful fellows may receive:

  • Up to US$550,000

The funding supports:

  • Research implementation
  • Career development
  • Training and academic growth
  • Research personnel and operational costs
  • International collaboration opportunities

Fellowship Duration

  • Four to five years

This long-term structure allows fellows to:

  • Conduct meaningful longitudinal research
  • Build sustainable research programmes
  • Strengthen institutional research capacity
  • Produce impactful academic and policy contributions

Program Structure

One of the fellowship’s unique strengths is its balance between local research leadership and international collaboration.

Home Institution-Based Research

Fellows remain based at their academic institutions within their home countries, ensuring that:

  • Research remains locally grounded
  • Projects respond directly to community needs
  • Local institutions benefit from strengthened research capacity

International Research Exposure

Fellows also gain opportunities for:

  • International stays
  • Collaboration with global experts
  • Exposure to international research networks
  • Cross-country learning and knowledge exchange

This structure ensures that projects are both:

  • Globally informed
  • Locally relevant

Research Focus Areas

The fellowship specifically supports projects related to:

  • Child mental health
  • Adolescent mental health
  • Youth well-being
  • Mental health systems strengthening
  • Psychological interventions
  • Mental health policy
  • Community mental health solutions
  • Prevention and early intervention research

The programme particularly values research that can:

  • Improve mental health outcomes for young people
  • Influence policy and practice
  • Generate scalable solutions
  • Address mental health inequities in LMICs

Why This Fellowship Matters

Mental health challenges among children and adolescents are increasing globally, especially in low-resource settings where:

  • Mental health services are limited
  • Research funding is scarce
  • Specialist professionals are insufficient
  • Mental health systems remain underdeveloped

The SNF Global Center Research Fellowship aims to address these gaps by investing directly in emerging researchers from LMICs.

The programme contributes to:

  • Building local research leadership
  • Expanding evidence-based mental health interventions
  • Strengthening institutional research ecosystems
  • Improving long-term mental health outcomes for young people globally

Institutional Nomination Requirement

An important feature of the fellowship is that applications must come through eligible institutions.

Key Requirement

  • Applicants cannot apply independently.
  • Candidates must first be nominated by an eligible academic institution.

Institutional Nomination Limits

  • Each eligible institution may nominate up to two candidates for the 2026 cohort.

Only nominated candidates will be invited to submit full fellowship applications.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the SNF Global Center Research Fellowship, applicants must:

Individual Requirements

  • Be an early-career researcher
  • Be affiliated with a leading academic institution in an LMIC
  • Conduct research related to child and adolescent mental health
  • Demonstrate strong research potential and leadership capacity
  • Be capable of serving as principal investigator for the proposed project

Institutional Requirements

  • Institutions must be located in eligible low- and middle-income countries
  • Institutions must formally nominate candidates
  • Institutions may nominate a maximum of two applicants

2026 Fellowship Timeline

Applicants and institutions should carefully note the official timeline for the 2026 fellowship cycle.

Important Dates

Launch for Nominations

  • Week of February 2, 2026

Webinar for Nominating Institutions

  • March 12, 2026 (9 AM EST)

Online Application Portal Opens

  • April 1, 2026

Webinar for Nominees

  • April 23, 2026 (9 AM EST)

Institutional Nomination Deadline

  • June 1, 2026

Full Application Deadline

  • June 15, 2026

Application Review Period

  • June – July 2026

Shortlisted Candidate Interviews

  • August 2026

Fellowship Announcement

  • October 10, 2026 (World Mental Health Day)

Deadline

The key deadlines for the 2026 SNF Global Center Research Fellowship are:

  • Institutional Nomination Deadline: June 1, 2026
  • Full Application Submission Deadline: June 15, 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin discussions with their institutions early due to the nomination requirement.

How to Apply

Interested researchers should:

  1. Contact their eligible academic institution
  2. Seek institutional nomination
  3. Prepare research proposal materials
  4. Submit the full application once invited

Applicants may also:

  • Review the Request for Applications
  • Attend informational webinars
  • Review Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Click here for more and to apply

Conclusion

The SNF Global Center Research Fellowship represents one of the most significant global mental health research opportunities currently available for early-career researchers in low- and middle-income countries.

By combining substantial funding, long-term career support, international collaboration, and locally grounded research leadership, the fellowship empowers scholars to develop transformative solutions that improve child and adolescent mental health outcomes worldwide.

For researchers committed to advancing mental health science and creating meaningful impact for young people, this fellowship offers a rare and highly prestigious opportunity.

Click here for more global fellowship opportunities

The post SNF Global Center Research Fellowship 2026: Up to $550,000 for Child and Adolescent Mental Health Research in LMICs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The Economic, Social and Cultural Council Registration 2026–2027: How African Civil Society Organizations Can Gain African Union Recognition and Participate in Continental Policy Dialogue
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The African Union’s Economic, Social and Cultural Council (ECOSOCC) has officially opened registration for organizations seeking to join the ECOSOCC Connect platform, a major continental initiative designed to strengthen the participation of African civil society and diaspora organizations in African Union policymaking and development processes. Through ECOSOCC Connect, organizations across Africa and the diaspora can […]

The post The Economic, Social and Cultural Council Registration 2026–2027: How African Civil Society Organizations Can Gain African Union Recognition and Participate in Continental Policy Dialogue appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Union’s Economic, Social and Cultural Council (ECOSOCC) has officially opened registration for organizations seeking to join the ECOSOCC Connect platform, a major continental initiative designed to strengthen the participation of African civil society and diaspora organizations in African Union policymaking and development processes.

Through ECOSOCC Connect, organizations across Africa and the diaspora can create official profiles, apply for accreditation, participate in African Union consultations, and contribute directly to continental dialogue on governance, development, social transformation, and public policy.

The platform serves as the recognized gateway for African civil society organizations to engage with the African Union and become part of a continent-wide network shaping Africa’s future.

In addition to the ongoing registration process, ECOSOCC has also announced applications for the 5th Permanent General Assembly (5PGA), a key programme aligned with the ECOSOCC Statute and official African Union participation requirements.

ECOSOCC Connect Registration and 5PGA Timeline

Registration Status

  • Open for applications and profile creation

5th Permanent General Assembly (5PGA)

  • Opening Date: April 28, 2026
  • Closing Date: October 29, 2027

Organizations interested in participating in the ECOSOCC General Assembly and broader AU policy activities are encouraged to complete registration early.

What Is ECOSOCC?

ECOSOCC, short for the Economic, Social and Cultural Council, is an official organ of the African Union created to facilitate the participation of African civil society in the work of the African Union.

The platform acts as the institutional bridge between:

  • African citizens
  • Civil society organizations
  • Policymakers
  • African Union institutions
  • Member states

ECOSOCC ensures that African civil society has a voice in continental decision-making and development strategies.

What Is ECOSOCC Connect?

ECOSOCC Connect is the African Union’s digital platform designed for:

  • Civil society registration
  • Accreditation management
  • Policy participation
  • Continental networking
  • Organizational visibility

The platform allows eligible organizations to:

  • Create official profiles
  • Register organizations online
  • Track accreditation processes
  • Access African Union opportunities
  • Participate in consultations and programmes

The initiative represents a major step toward improving transparency, accessibility, and inclusion in African Union civic engagement processes.

Why ECOSOCC Connect Matters for African Civil Society

Civil society organizations play a critical role in advancing democracy, human rights, youth empowerment, development, gender equality, education, public health, and governance across Africa.

However, many organizations face barriers when attempting to engage directly with continental institutions and policymaking spaces.

ECOSOCC Connect addresses this challenge by creating a centralized and officially recognized platform through which organizations can interact with the African Union.

The platform gives organizations:

  • Visibility
  • Recognition
  • Participation opportunities
  • Policy engagement channels
  • Access to continental networks

Key Benefits of Registering on ECOSOCC Connect

Organizations that register on the platform gain multiple strategic advantages.

1. Continental Recognition

Registered organizations receive formal recognition within the African Union ecosystem.

This recognition strengthens:

  • Organizational credibility
  • Institutional legitimacy
  • Regional visibility
  • Partnership opportunities

It also demonstrates that the organization is actively engaged in continental development and civic participation.

2. Participation in African Union Activities

Accredited organizations can apply to participate in:

  • Consultations
  • Policy dialogues
  • Programmes
  • AU-led initiatives
  • Continental events
  • Development discussions

This creates direct opportunities for organizations to influence policymaking processes at the continental level.

3. Inclusion in the Official Continental CSO Database

Registered organizations are listed in the official African Union civil society database.

This improves visibility among:

  • African Union organs
  • AU member states
  • Development agencies
  • Regional institutions
  • Potential collaborators and partners

4. Streamlined Online Accreditation

ECOSOCC Connect modernizes and simplifies the accreditation process.

Organizations can:

  • Submit applications online
  • Upload required documents digitally
  • Track application progress
  • Receive guided submission support

This removes many administrative barriers traditionally associated with accreditation processes.

5. Voice in African Union Policy Processes

One of the most important benefits of ECOSOCC accreditation is policy engagement.

Organizations can contribute expertise and recommendations through:

  • Sectoral Cluster Committees
  • Policy consultations
  • Continental discussions
  • Advocacy platforms

This ensures that civil society perspectives are integrated into African Union decision-making processes.

Who Can Register on ECOSOCC Connect?

The platform is open to a broad range of African civil society and diaspora organizations.

Eligible Organizations Include:

1. African Civil Society Organizations (CSOs)

Organizations operating within African Union member states can apply for registration and accreditation.

Examples include:

  • Nonprofits
  • Advocacy organizations
  • Community groups
  • NGOs
  • Research organizations
  • Youth organizations
  • Women-led organizations
  • Human rights groups

2. African Diaspora Organizations

Organizations representing African diaspora communities worldwide are also eligible.

This allows Africans living outside the continent to participate in AU policy conversations and development initiatives.

3. Organizations Operating in AU Member States

Eligible organizations must be:

  • Registered
  • Operational
  • Active within one or more of the 55 African Union member states or the diaspora

African Union Member States Covered

ECOSOCC Connect serves organizations across all 55 African Union member states, including:

  • Algeria
  • Angola
  • Benin
  • Botswana
  • Burkina Faso
  • Burundi
  • Cabo Verde
  • Cameroon
  • Central African Republic
  • Chad
  • Comoros
  • Congo
  • Democratic Republic of Congo
  • Côte d’Ivoire
  • Djibouti
  • Egypt
  • Equatorial Guinea
  • Eritrea
  • Eswatini
  • Ethiopia
  • Gabon
  • Gambia
  • Ghana
  • Guinea
  • Guinea-Bissau
  • Kenya
  • Lesotho
  • Liberia
  • Libya
  • Madagascar
  • Malawi
  • Mali
  • Mauritania
  • Mauritius
  • Morocco
  • Mozambique
  • Namibia
  • Niger
  • Nigeria
  • Rwanda
  • Sahrawi Republic
  • São Tomé and Príncipe
  • Senegal
  • Seychelles
  • Sierra Leone
  • Somalia
  • South Africa
  • South Sudan
  • Sudan
  • Tanzania
  • Togo
  • Tunisia
  • Uganda
  • Zambia
  • Zimbabwe

About the 5th Permanent General Assembly (5PGA)

One of the major opportunities currently available through ECOSOCC Connect is participation in the 5th Permanent General Assembly (5PGA).

The 5PGA serves as:

  • A continental civil society engagement platform
  • A governance and representation mechanism
  • A framework for structured AU-CSO collaboration

The application cycle is aligned with:

  • The ECOSOCC Statute
  • Official application requirements
  • AU governance standards

Organizations interested in deeper continental participation are encouraged to apply.

Languages Available on the Platform

To ensure inclusivity and accessibility across Africa and the diaspora, ECOSOCC Connect is available in multiple languages, including:

  • English
  • French
  • Arabic
  • Kiswahili
  • Portuguese
  • Spanish

This multilingual approach helps organizations from diverse regions participate more effectively.

Frequently Asked Questions About ECOSOCC Connect

Does My Organization Need to Be Based in Africa?

No. African diaspora organizations outside the continent are also eligible.

Can Organizations Less Than Three Years Old Apply?

The platform includes eligibility guidance regarding organizational maturity and accreditation requirements. Applicants are encouraged to review the official criteria carefully.

Can Applications Be Edited After Submission?

The platform includes online tracking and guided submission systems that support organizations throughout the process.

What Documents Are Required?

Organizations will typically need:

  • Registration certificates
  • Organizational profiles
  • Governance documents
  • Supporting institutional information

Applicants should consult the official ECOSOCC guidelines for complete requirements.

Why Organizations Should Join ECOSOCC Connect

Joining ECOSOCC Connect provides organizations with:

  • Continental visibility
  • African Union recognition
  • Access to policy discussions
  • Networking opportunities
  • Governance participation
  • Advocacy platforms
  • Institutional credibility

For organizations committed to shaping Africa’s future, ECOSOCC Connect offers a direct pathway into continental dialogue and decision-making.

Official Registration and Application Links

Organizations can register and apply through the official ECOSOCC Connect platform.

Official Website: ECOSOCC Connect Platform

Create Profile: ECOSOCC Registration Portal

Discover more global membership opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post The Economic, Social and Cultural Council Registration 2026–2027: How African Civil Society Organizations Can Gain African Union Recognition and Participate in Continental Policy Dialogue appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: William T. Grant Foundation Institutional Challenge Grant 2026: Up to $650,000 to Strengthen Research-Practice Partnerships and Reduce Youth Inequality in the United States
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The William T. Grant Foundation has officially launched applications for its highly competitive 2026 Institutional Challenge Grant, a transformative funding opportunity aimed at helping research institutions and public-serving organizations build long-term partnerships that improve youth outcomes across the United States.
